phases.label: Primaries & Caucuses
phases.description: Parties select their nominees for the general election.
voterActions: Register for your party (in closed states)

candidateDetails.filing: Requirements vary by state (Signature petitions vs Fees)
candidateDetails.fec: Must register with FEC once $5,000 is raised/spent
candidateDetails.delegates: Must reach threshold in state primaries to win delegates

phases.label: Voter Registration
phases.description: Registering to vote in the general election.
voterActions: Check ID requirements for your state

eligibility: U.S. resident for at least 14 years

phases.label: Early Voting & Mail-in
phases.description: Many states allow voting before Election Day.
steps: Drop off ballots at secure boxes
In-person early voting at designated centers

candidateDetails.strategy: GOTV (Get Out The Vote) programs for early voters

phases.label: Election Day
phases.description: The main day for in-person voting across the country.
voterActions: Know your rights if you're in line when polls close

phases.label: Electoral College
phases.description: The formal process of electing the President.
steps: Electors cast votes in their states

glossary.definition: A body of electors chosen to elect the President and VP.
An election to select a party's candidate.
A meeting of party members to select candidates.
A ballot completed and typically mailed in advance of an election.

quickQuestions: How do I register to vote?
What ID do I need to vote?

quiz.question: What is the day the US Presidential Election is held?
options: First Tuesday after the first Monday of November
quiz.explanation: Election Day is the Tuesday after the first Monday in November.

quiz.question: How many Electoral College votes are needed to win the Presidency?
quiz.explanation: A candidate must win a majority of electoral votes (270 out of 538) to become President.

quiz.question: Which body officially certifies the Electoral College results?
quiz.explanation: Congress meets in a joint session to count electoral votes and certify the result.

quiz.question: What is Super Tuesday?
options: The day the President is inaugurated
The day many states hold primary elections
The first day of early voting
The day the budget is passed
quiz.explanation: Super Tuesday is the day when the largest number of U.S. states hold primary elections or caucuses.
